title: Colman Domingo Rips Elon Musk’s Plan for AI ‘The Odyssey’ Movie: ‘It’ll Be ‘Whiter Than Christmas at Cracker Barrel’
description:
Colman Domingo slammed Elon Musk‘s plan for an AI-generated version of “The Odyssey.”

	“By historically accurate, he means whiter than Christmas at
body:
Colman Domingo slammed Elon Musk‘s plan for an AI-generated version of “The Odyssey.”

	“By historically accurate, he means whiter than Christmas at Cracker Barrel,” Domingo joked during his stint as guest host on “Jimmy Kimmel Live!” on Wednesday night. “Of course, this is all because Elon and his Musketeers are upset over the diversity casting of ‘The Odyssey.'”

	Domingo centered much of his opening monologue on Musk, who vowed that before the end of the year, his AI tool Grok “will make a full-length movie of The Odyssey that is historically accurate and true to the art of Homer.” Musk also shared a three-minute clip of an AI-generated scene adapted from the poem, which was manufactured by a user on Grok.
	

	“Of course, this is all because Elon and his Musketeers are upset over the diversity casting of ‘The Odyssey,” Domingo said. “And I don’t want to get technical here, but you can’t make a historically accurate version of something that is totally made the fuck up.”


	Domingo is the latest in the industry to mock Musk’s recent crusade against Nolan’s adaptation. Earlier this week, the “Doctor Strange” director Scott Derrickson addressed Musk directly on X, tweeting “Clearly @elonmusk doesn’t know shit about cinema & he stopped reading in his 20s.”

	Musk has railed against Nolan’s “Odyssey” long before it was released due to his perception of certain “woke” casting decisions. In March, the tech mogul wrote that “Chris Nolan is an anti-White racist” for casting Lupita Nyong’o in the role of Helen of Troy, said “Chris Nolan has lost his integrity” and called the casting an “insult” to Homer.

schema.headline: Colman Domingo Rips Elon Musk’s Plan for AI ‘The Odyssey’ Movie: ‘It’ll Be ‘Whiter Than Christmas at Cracker Barrel’

title: Lilly Wachowski Boards Alexandra Grey’s Sci-Fi Short ‘The Day Before’ as Executive Producer (EXCLUSIVE)
description: “The Matrix” and “Sense8” co-creator Lilly Wachowski has boarded “The Day Before,” an indie sci-fi short written, directed and starring Alexandra Gr
body:
“The Matrix” and “Sense8” co-creator Lilly Wachowski has boarded “The Day Before,” an indie sci-fi short written, directed and starring Alexandra Grey.

	Grey leads an ensemble cast that includes Matthew Law (“Nemesis,” “Abbott Elementary”), Jamie Clayton (“Sense8,” “Hellraiser”) and Alexander David Pleasant (“Abbott Elementary”).

	Set on the eve of NASA’s most ambitious space mission, “The Day Before” follows Dr. Erynne Washington (Grey), the first astronaut selected for a historic one-way journey to Mars. As the world celebrates her achievement, Erynne spends one final day on Earth with her husband, Marcus (Law), and their young son, Noah (Pleasant), knowing she will never return home.
	

	Clayton plays news anchor Caroline Shaw (Clayton), who sits down with Erynne for her final televised interview. The short recently wrapped production in Los Angeles and will be positioned for a 2027 festival premiere.


	“The Day Before” is produced by Grey under her banner Bishop Street Production, as well as two-time Emmy nominated producer Zackary Drucker (“The Stroll,” “Transparent”), Jonathan Olson and Student Academy Award winning producer Ilayda Cetinkaya.

	In addition to Wachowski, executive producers include Jalen Jones, Grafton Doyle and John Reyes Doyle of Equites Productions and TransLatin@ Coalition president and CEO Bamby Salcedo.

	Grey is a writer, director, and actress whose credits include “Empire,” “How to Get Away with Murder,” “MacGyver,” “The Resident” and “The Alienist.” She is represented by Zero Gravity Management, GVA Talent Agency and Del Shaw Moonves Tanaka Finkelstein & Lezcano.
	

	Law currently stars in Netflix’s “Nemesis” and is represented by Buchwald and AVG Management. Clayton is represented by Neon Kite and Toczek Suddleson Law Group. Pleasant is represented by the Daniel Hoff Agency and Top Ten Talent Entertainment.

schema.headline: Lilly Wachowski Boards Alexandra Grey’s Sci-Fi Short ‘The Day Before’ as Executive Producer (EXCLUSIVE)

title: Xbox Is Asking Customers to Volunteer to Test Ad-Supported Game Streaming
description: Xbox is rolling out a test for ad-supported cloud gaming that will be available on a voluntary basis for members of its fan-feedback community, Xbox
body:
Xbox is rolling out a test for ad-supported cloud gaming that will be available on a voluntary basis for members of its fan-feedback community, Xbox Insiders.

	Beginning Thursday, Xbox Insiders with an Xbox account will have the option to try out the free, ad-supported experience via a selection of games the players already own, including “Forza Horizon 6” and “007 First Light,” or through free-to-play games like “Call of Duty: Warzone,” “Genshin Impact,” and “War Thunder.” 
	

	While Xbox has not confirmed this test will officially lead to it offering a future ad-supported tier of cloud gaming, the company states “we’ll learn from this test and continue to improve it based on how players use it and what they tell us.”


	More than 1,800 games in the Xbox Store support cloud game streaming, though it should be noted the Xbox Insiders ad test experience does not offer players access to all available games — just those titles the user already owns or that are free-to-play.

	During the test, players will see a two-minute block of multiple ads before joining the cloud streaming queue to play their selected game. Variety has confirmed the ads will be from globally recognizable brands and specific to the markets that Xbox Insiders are viewing them within.

	Players will then have a one-hour game play session that’s uninterrupted by ad breaks but includes countdown warnings. If at the end of the session a player wants to continue, they can select to watch a new block of ads before resuming gameplay.

	As Xbox cloud gaming is currently not available without a GamePass subscription, the ad test experience will allow Xbox Insiders who do not have GamePass to stream their already owned games. Through the ad-supported experience, if users don’t have a console and don’t have a GamePass subscription, they can play their library games or Xbox Store’s free-to-play games on cloud streaming via handheld, PC or the Xbox app on TV.
	

	“For many players, the device they already own now allows them to stream games with Xbox,” Xbox said in the Xbox Wire blog post announcing the test Thursday. “Cloud can also help Xbox One owners play newer games built for Xbox Series X|S without needing to purchase new hardware right away. That can be especially meaningful in places where consoles are harder to find or less affordable, giving more players another way to access great games on devices they already have.”

	“Advertising has existed in gaming for decades, from in-game placements to free-to-play models,” the blog post continues. “But it hasn’t always been built with the player in mind. When done well, advertising can help lower the cost of access. It creates another way for players to get into games without an upfront streaming purchase and expands audiences through new ways to play.”

	Additionally, Xbox laid out these five ways it plans to “approach ad-supported services across Xbox” moving forward:


We start with the player. Ads should create value.
We do not interrupt the flow of gameplay.
We hold ads to the same quality bar as our content.
We’re clear about what’s advertising.
We make sure ads fit what players expect on each platform.



	Existing Xbox Insiders can start the ad-supported test experience now and players who aren’t part of the program yet can join today by downloading the Xbox Insider Hub for Xbox Series X and S and Xbox One or Windows PC.

schema.headline: Xbox Is Asking Customers to Volunteer to Test Ad-Supported Game Streaming

title: Athletes and Execs Join Forces at Variety’s Inaugural Sports and Entertainment Studio, Featuring Russell Wilson, Jimmie Johnson and Jay Williams
description: As the worlds of entertainment and sports continue to overlap, Variety held its inaugural Sports & Entertainment Studio in New York, featuring Super
body:
As the worlds of entertainment and sports continue to overlap, Variety held its inaugural Sports & Entertainment Studio in New York, featuring Super Bowl-winning quarterback Russell Wilson, NASCAR legend Jimmie Johnson and ESPN studio analyst Jay Williams, as well as top executives from State Farm, PepsiCo and Teads.

	On July 16, Variety reporters Joe Otterson, Marlow Stern and Brian Steinberg led conversations in the New York City studio about the players’ and executives’ respective careers, growing sport fandoms, and how networks and brands can collaborate and connect to better reach consumers during an explosive time for media.
	

	Top players like Wilson are often tapped to provide commentary and expertise for weekly programs, such as CBS Studios’ “The NFL Today,” giving fans an inside look into gameplay strategy and the reality of playing in high-pressure games aired around the world. But they also recognize the current missed opportunities in the marketplace.

	Read below for a recap of Variety‘s conversations with the leaders across sports and entertainment.

	Russell Wilson, Super Bowl Champion Quarterback, Entrepreneur, Sports Owner and Philanthropist 

	Russell Wilson has had quite the busy year, announcing his retirement from the NFL in June to move to CBS Sports as a broadcaster for the network’s Sunday pregame show. The record-breaking quarterback’s varied career allows his commentary to tap into what is really going on in a player’s mind. “I don’t think there’s anything I can’t talk about, meaning I’ve had all the highs, I’ve had some of the lows,” Wilson said. “I’ve won a Super Bowl, I’ve also been in an unfortunate situation of not being able to win one. So all those experiences I think you use.”
	

	Wilson compares being a quarterback to the responsibility a CEO of any organization might face. “You’re constantly speaking to people, but also, too, you’re constantly leading people,” he said. “When you come into the huddle, there’s ten other guys in the huddle. And everybody has different socioeconomic statuses. Everybody’s a different size, everybody has different years and experience. Everybody has different talents.”

	Wilson also spoke about how his work with CBS Sports allows him to tip his hat to the game and say thank you, both to his biggest fans and to those who doubted him along the way. “To be the second Black quarterback to ever win a Super Bowl, that’s history,” he reflected while speaking to his accomplishments.

	Jimmie Johnson, 7-time NASCAR Cup Champion and Team Owner, Legacy Motor Club 

	Jimmie Johnson found himself accomplishing many of his dreams early on in his NASCAR racing career, from landing a Cup Series job to winning a race himself. Now at the age of 50, Johnson has joined TNT Sports as a studio analyst while still racing in select events.

	“Our sport has done an incredible job of creating parity and equality on the track. I think there’s been a lot of variables over the years thrown at motor sports in general,” Johnson said, noting that from 2010 to 2020 there was a significant decline in motor sports’ popularity. “But as it turns out, timing is in a really good place for NASCAR. We’re still the second-largest viewed sport in America and the largest attended. And so although we’re not where our peak once was — I think the only exception to that is the NFL — [considering] all other sports and how the consumer has changed the way they digest anything, NASCAR is holding its own.”

	While NASCAR races thrive on fans wanting to experience their favorite drivers go head-to-head in person, Johnson said the industry “is a little behind” on staying connected through different forms of media: “We see these reality TV shows pop up in sports and out. People want to look behind the curtain, and finding the right creative storytelling is everything. And that’s how you capture someone on their device, it’s how you get them to watch on television, that’s how you get them to the event.”

	Kristyn Cook, EVP and Chief Agency, Marketing and Sales Officer, State Farm; and Carolyn Braff, Head of Sports and Entertainment, PepsiCo
	

	As top executives at State Farm and PepsiCo, Kristyn Cook and Carolyn Braff know they have to meet consumers where they’re at. So naturally, they get into the business of sports, with milestone events like the World Cup and Super Bowl providing extra incentives to buy out ad space and collaborate with various teams.

	“It is becoming harder and harder to reach big groups of consumers at the same time,” Braff said. “You have to find places where you’ve got large swaths of people paying attention. Sports is a great place to do that. Now, once you’ve got that huge group of consumers, you have to talk to them as individuals and not a large swath.”

	Cook noted that State Farm has found success leaning into fan connections with their favorite athletes, including partnerships with Patrick Mahomes from the NFL and Chris Paul from the NBA. “People really tune into the athletes, and we’re there for it,” Cook said.

	Jay Williams, ESPN Personality / Entrepreneur & Investor 

	When Jay Williams’ NBA career was halted back in 2003 due to a near-fatal motorcycle accident, he quickly had to learn the art of pivoting as a businessman. Now, as he sees “sports valuations” continuing to rise, the ESPN studio analyst and media personality is focused on trying to turn Las Vegas into the “Mecca” of basketball.

	“I don’t expect anybody to give anything to me because of the color of my skin,” Williams said of his desire to pursue an NBA expansion franchise, noting the achievements of previous Black owners like Magic Johnson, Michael Jordan and Grant Hill. “I want to actually do it on the merit of my character and actually the capital that we have raised behind what our overall value proposition is to the league.”

	Now, Williams finds himself going up against the likes of former Disney CEO Bob Iger and Thrive Capital CEO Joshua Kushner, but he’s “always open” to healthy competition. “The road has been laid out for more athletes and entertainers who have played this sport to have a seat at the table as an owner,” he added. “And I hope that we’re able to get the ball over the finish line.”

	Dani Cushion, Chief Marketing Officer at Teads

	As a top executive at Teads, an omnichannel ad platform that helps brands bring their stories to life, Dani Cushion recognizes the natural impulse for advertisers to focus on major events like the Super Bowl and the World Cup.
	

	Cushion sees that as a missed opportunity to build something more sustainable with the passionate sports fans who stick around all year long. “You actually do have better value if you’re building a relationship before, during, after and throughout major tournament,” she said, noting how it can be “expensive” to buy an ad for just one specific campaign.

	Companies like Teads also have the data to prove that as consumer behavior evolves, most sports fans are now watching games at home — or even on a second screen while multi-tasking. “It’s really about thinking how those screens work together to surround the consumer with a great story that’s going to break through,” Cushion added.

schema.headline: Athletes and Execs Join Forces at Variety’s Inaugural Sports and Entertainment Studio, Featuring Russell Wilson, Jimmie Johnson and Jay Williams

title: Critics Choice Association Launches Inaugural Creator Awards for 2027, Honoring Podcasts, Micro-Dramas and Digital Stars
description:
The Critics Choice Awards are getting into the creator business.

	The organization announced it will hold the inaugural Critics Choice Creator Awar
body:
The Critics Choice Awards are getting into the creator business.

	The organization announced it will hold the inaugural Critics Choice Creator Awards, honoring the finest creators in the digital space across podcasts, scripted, unscripted, documentary, informational, lifestyle, sports and game series. The inaugural nominees will be announced March 18, with winners unveiled April 15.

	The ceremony aims to bring together the biggest names in the creator community, from established stars like MrBeast, Dhar Mann and Rhett and Link to up-and-comers such as Nicky Campbell, Grace Reiter and Eric Sedeño, all of whom were featured on Variety’s inaugural “10 Creators to Watch.” It will also recognize podcasters including Jay Shetty, Steven Bartlett and Amy Poehler, who won the Golden Globes’ inaugural podcast award at last year’s ceremony, as well as the growing world of micro-dramas like the TikTok thriller “Screen Time” from Issa Rae.
	

	“As the world of entertainment evolves and migrates onto social media and streaming platforms, it is the job of entertainment critics to move with them,” CCA president Joey Berlin said in a statement. “We are dedicated to helping entertainment consumers ‘find the good stuff,’ as well as helping the best creators get found and appreciated.”


	The Critics Choice Creator Awards joins CCA’s robust slate of programming, which already includes the main event Critics Choice Awards, along with the Critics Choice Documentary Awards, Critics Choice Real TV Awards and Critics Choice Super Awards. The CCA also produces a series of Celebration events to recognize historically underrepresented communities, including the Celebration of Black Cinema and Television, Celebración of Cinema and Television, Celebration of AAPI Cinema and Television and Celebration of LGBTQ+ Cinema and Television.
	

	The official categories and timeline are listed below.



	

	



	Critics Choice Creator Awards Categories:

	General Awards


Best Creator of the Year
Best Live Streaming Creator
Best Video Edit
Best Viral Video
Best Special Effects



	Podcast


Best Audio Podcast
Best Video Podcast
Best Podcast Host



	Genre Awards


Best Comedy Creator
Best Drama Creator
Best Storyteller
Best News Reporter



	Scripted Series


Best Comedy Series: Short Form (Under 3 Minutes)
Best Comedy Series: Long Form (3+ Minutes)
Best Micro-Drama Series: Short Form (Under 3 Minutes)
Best Micro-Drama Series: Long Form (3+ Minutes)
Best Actor in a Comedy Series
Best Actress in a Comedy Series
Best Actor in a Micro-Drama Series
Best Actress in a Micro-Drama Series



	Unscripted & Reality


Best Competition Series
Best Competition Series: Talent/Variety
Best Structured Series
Best Series Host



	Documentary & Informational


Best Documentary Series
Best Documentary Creator
Best Educational Series
Best Educational Creator
Best Business Series
Best Business Creator
Best Crime/Justice Series
Best Crime/Justice Creator
Best Animal/Nature Series
Best Animal/Nature Creator
Best Health/Wellness Series
Best Health/Wellness Creator



	Lifestyle & Interest


Best Food & Drink Series
Best Food & Drink Creator
Best Travel/Adventure Series
Best Travel/Adventure Creator
Best Relationship Series
Best Relationship Series Creator
Best Lifestyle Series: Home/Garden
Best Lifestyle Creator: Home/Garden
Best Lifestyle Series: Fashion/Beauty
Best Lifestyle Creator: Fashion/Beauty
Best Pop Culture Series
Best Pop Culture Creator



	Games


Best Game Show
Best Game Show Creator



	Sports


Best Sports Show: Talk/Analysis
Best Sports Series



	Critics Choice Creator Awards Timeline:


Monday, October 19, 2026 – Early Submissions Open
Monday, November 16, 2026 – Early Submissions Close; Regular Submissions Open
Monday, February 1, 2027 – Regular Submissions Close
Tuesday, February 2 – Friday, March 12, 2027 – NomCom Deliberations
Thursday, March 18, 2027 – Nominees Announced
Thursday, April 15, 2027 – Critics Choice Creator Awards Winners Announced

schema.headline: Critics Choice Association Launches Inaugural Creator Awards for 2027, Honoring Podcasts, Micro-Dramas and Digital Stars

title: ‘Morning Show’ to End With Season 5 at Apple TV
description: The sun is setting on “The Morning Show.” Variety has learned that the Apple TV drama will end with its upcoming fifth season. It is slated to debut
body:
The sun is setting on “The Morning Show.” Variety has learned that the Apple TV drama will end with its upcoming fifth season. It is slated to debut in 2027.

	This will mark the end of an era at Apple TV, as “The Morning Show” was one of the very first Apple scripted original series. Apple initially gave it a two-season order in 2017, two years before the streamer officially launched, with Jennifer Aniston and Reese Witherspoon attached in the lead roles. 
	

	“From the beginning, Apple has supported our vision for ‘The Morning Show,’ and we feel so lucky to have had the opportunity to tell stories that reflect what’s happening in the world around us with a twist of dark humor,” said Aniston. “It’s been such a privilege to work with this insanely talented cast, and our extraordinary writers, producers, and crew. We’ve become such a family and we’re all so excited to share this final season. And going in knowing it would be our last, we were so fortunate to craft the conclusion with intentionality, and honor these characters the way they deserve.”


	“The past nine years producing and acting in ‘The Morning Show’ have been the honor of a lifetime,” added Witherspoon. “This was always more than a show about a newsroom — it was a show about why the newsroom matters. About freedom of the press, and the journalists who protect the fourth estate, often at great cost. Telling that story has been the creative privilege of my career. Thank you to Apple for their unwavering support, to our visionary writers, our incredible crew, and our extraordinarily talented cast, who brought five seasons to life with a truth audiences believed every step of the way. It has been a stellar creative experience — one I will always treasure.”
	

	Aside from Aniston and Witherspoon, the cast for the series includes: Billy Crudup, Mark Duplass, Nestor Carbonell, Karen Pittman, Nicole Beharie and Jon Hamm, along with new additions for season five including Jeff Daniels, Reneé Rapp, Jesse Williams, Sean Hayes, Lizzy Caplan and more. 

	Charlotte Stoudt serves as showrunner and executive producer on “The Morning Show.” Mimi Leder serves as director and executive producer. Aniston executive produces via Echo Films along with Kristin Hahn, while Witherspoon executive produces under her Hello Sunshine banner along with Lauren Neustadter. Michael Ellenberg of Media Res also executive produces, as do Zander Lehmann and Micah Schraft. The series is produced by Media Res for Apple TV. Both Leder and Stoudt remain under overall deals with Apple. 

	“Our first order was an important one, and seeing this incredible Apple TV series through from start to finish has been the joy of a lifetime and career,” said Zack Van Amburg, Apple’s head of Worldwide Video. “Jennifer, Reese, Michael and Mimi not only created a series that continually challenged audiences, sparked conversation and stood at the forefront of television over five unforgettable seasons, they also helped launch an entirely new streaming platform with their signature series. ‘The Morning Show’ team consists of the best cast and crew in television, led by Charlotte Stoudt’s exceptional writing and showrunning. As we celebrate this final chapter, while also beginning new ones with these brilliant artists and storytellers, we’re endlessly grateful to everyone who made ‘The Morning Show’ such an enduring part of Apple TV.”

	“Working alongside storytellers like Jennifer, Reese, Michael, Mimi and Charlotte has been one of the great privileges of building Apple TV,” said Jamie Erlicht, Apple’s Head of Worldwide Video. “Their extraordinary creative partnership has made ‘The Morning Show’ one of the defining series of our time. We’re profoundly grateful to the artists who poured their talent and passion into this remarkable series, and incredibly proud that the impact of ‘The Morning Show’ will continue to resonate long after its final season.”

	Season 4 of “The Morning Show” launched in September 2025. It was set in the spring of 2024, almost two years after the events of Season 3. The official description for the season states, “With the UBA-NBN merger complete, the newsroom must grapple with newfound responsibility, hidden motives and the elusive nature of truth in a polarized America. In a world rife with deepfakes, conspiracy theories and corporate cover-ups — who can you trust? And how can you know what’s actually real?” 
	

	“‘The Morning Show’ is the ultimate labor of love for everyone involved, from the writers, to the crew, to the directors, to the cast, led by our incredible partners, Jen and Reese, who from the very beginning set the course for the fearless, timely, provocative, ambitious and rousing show we’ve poured our hearts and souls into these last years,” said Ellenberg, CEO and founder of Media Res. “The show has always embraced big swings, and our incredible showrunner Charlotte Stoudt, and our legendary producing director Mimi Leder, have kept pushing boundaries season to season. We’ve been blessed to connect with a large global audience, and we can’t wait for fans to see the fifth and final season of this beautiful journey. On behalf of everyone at Media Res, thank you to our extraordinary crew, our staggering, glittering cast, our remarkable writers, and to our partners at Apple, Hello Sunshine and Echo Films. This remarkable producing team has become a tight family – and we look forward to sharing this final season with our extended TMS family worldwide.”

	“The Morning Show” has been a mainstay on the awards circuit since it launched. The show has received 28 Emmy nominations to date with four wins, including two for Crudup as outstanding supporting actor in a drama. The show has also been nominated for best drama series, while both Aniston and Witherspoon have been nominated for their performances. 

	Witherspoon is also an executive producer on the Apple TV series “Lucky” with Anya Taylor-Joy, while Aniston and Echo Films are continuing to develop projects at the streamer. Hamm is also the star and executive producer of the Apple series “Your Friends and Neighbors.”

	“For five seasons, these messy, resilient journalists have chased their dreams. Now we’ll see where their endeavors lead them — what story will they ultimately choose for themselves,” said Stoudt. “It’s been a thrill and a privilege to write for this generous, extraordinary cast.”

	“It has been the honor and the privilege of my career to birth this series from conception to its final conclusion with this brilliant cast, stellar crew, and the extraordinary writers who, through our indelible characters, put a microscope on our world, and implored our duty to speak truth to power to protect the freedom of the press at all costs,” said Leder. “It’s been a beautiful and meaningful journey.”

schema.headline: ‘Morning Show’ to End With Season 5 at Apple TV

title: Oscar Voters Rave Over Christopher Nolan’s ‘The Odyssey’ at First Academy Screenings: ‘The Definitive Filmmaker of Our Time’
description:
“What was the song of the sirens like?”

	When it comes to Oscar voters, like Odysseus, the answer is: “All the things you wanted to be.”

	The Osca
body:
“What was the song of the sirens like?”

	When it comes to Oscar voters, like Odysseus, the answer is: “All the things you wanted to be.”

	The Oscar race for 2027 seems to have found its center of gravity, and its front-runner that will be shepherded under the Universal banner. Christopher Nolan‘s “The Odyssey,” the Homer adaptation that shattered the director’s own global opening record with $264 million worldwide, began its formal courtship of Academy voters this week with a series of member screenings on both coasts and abroad. If the early temperature reading is correct, and the buzz holds, the film enters the fall corridor as the one everybody else has to beat.
	

	The Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences hosted its first members screening on Saturday at the Samuel Goldwyn Theater in Los Angeles, presented in 70mm. The roughly 1,000-seat house was near capacity, an early tell in a season where turnout becomes its own form of polling. The U.K. membership, which has become an instrumental voting bloc as AMPAS has gone more international, followed on Monday at the Soho Hotel. And for members in New York City, they enjoyed the journey back to Ithaca on Wednesday in 35mm. 


	Not surprisingly, the reactions from several members who were in attendance were borderline reverential.

	“I didn’t think anything could top ‘Oppenheimer’ and Chris’ [Nolan] first film Following,’” says one veteran Academy member in attendance. “He is the definitive filmmaker of our time.”

	That kind of enthusiasm and language matters. Nolan has spent the better part of two decades as the most commercially dominant filmmaker the Oscars could not quite bring themselves to reward, until the 2024 historical biopic “Oppenheimer” swept its season and took home best picture and gave him his first directing statuette. Voters do not typically hand a filmmaker multiple coronations, especially in close succession. They do, however, reward a body of work that keeps elevating, and “The Odyssey,” the first feature shot entirely on Imax cameras, is elevated by design.
	

	Not everyone came away without any reservations, though the caveats were still incredibly affectionate rather than damaging.

	“I thought it was spectacular,” another anonymous Academy voter says. “A little bit long but massive in scale.”



	

									Melinda Sue Gordon
	



	The most consequential conversation will be happening inside of the actors branch, the largest voting bloc in the Academy and the one that decides which performances graduate from admired to nominated. Matt Damon leads an ensemble that includes Tom Holland, Anne Hathaway, Zendaya, Lupita Nyong’o, Robert Pattinson, Charlize Theron, John Leguizamo and Jon Bernthal. At least one branch member who skipped the official screening and bought a ticket instead came away doing the math on the rest of the calendar.

	“This was like the NBA All-Star game of acting,” the actor says. “Matt [Damon], both ‘Johns’ [Bernthal and Leguizamo], Rob [Pattinson], Anne [Hathaway] were incredible. Good luck to those of us that have movies coming out the rest of the year.”

	Ensembles of this size historically cannibalize their own campaigns, splitting supporting votes into confetti. But a lead is a different equation, and Damon, who has one competitive Oscar for co-writing “Good Will Hunting” (1997) and two acting nominations across a 30-year career — “Invictus” and “The Martian” — is suddenly the beneficiary of the biggest opening of his life.

	A veteran artisan who attended describes crying twice, and the two moments they cite are telling, because neither is a battle sequence.

	“I became so emotional when he [Matt Damon] hears the Sirens, and when he pets his beautiful dog,” the member says. “I think Matt has to win the Oscar. He has to.”

	Argos the dog’s scene landing that hard is also providing the detailed campaign that strategists dream about. Spectacle gets a film nominated, but intimacy and emotion inside them is what gets performers recognized, and a movie ultimately crowned.

	Variety already offered its thoughts about how well this film could perform during the awards season. While anyone with eyes and ears can feel the passion and overwhelming adoration for the mythological epic, it further cements those feelings when you hear from the voters who will decide how well this will do.
	

	But there is a long road ahead. Telluride, Venice and Toronto are still to come, with multiple dramas, comedies, genres and under-the-radar projects that could offer “The Odyssey” a viable challenger. And while the fall prestige slate has not been revealed, the Academy membership has a habit of falling for a summer release in July and then being distracted by new, shiny objects in January. Nolan’s own “Dunkirk,” a summer release with eight nominations, lost best picture to a December fable by Guillermo del Toro.

	Still, the difference between admiration and momentum is usually measured in whether members show up, and whether they talk afterward. This week, according to various sources, they did both.

schema.headline: Oscar Voters Rave Over Christopher Nolan’s ‘The Odyssey’ at First Academy Screenings: ‘The Definitive Filmmaker of Our Time’

title: The Roku Channel Expands Partnership With Create the Writers Room Initiative by Streaming ‘Five Film Fridays’ Selections
description: Create the Writers Room, a creative incubator for underrepresented storytellers, is expanding its ongoing partnership with the Roku Channel. Select
body:
Create the Writers Room, a creative incubator for underrepresented storytellers, is expanding its ongoing partnership with the Roku Channel. Select films from CTWR’s “Five Film Fridays” are streaming the Roku Channel, giving the filmmakers and talent exposure to the platform’s 100 million subscribers.

	Create The Writers Room was founded in 2018 by filmmaker Rickey Castleberry with the mission to provide filmmakers with exhibition opportunities, as well as to equip them with the tools, education and industry insight necessary to retain creative control and build sustainable careers. “Five Film Fridays” is CTWR’s bespoke screening series: each quarter, five standout short films or proof-of-concepts are handpicked by industry insiders and showcased. The initiative also features intimate panels and fireside chats centered on ownership, authorship and navigating the evolving entertainment landscape.
	

	“I was inspired to create Five Film Fridays as an outlet for creatives to network, build community, and truly understand the business behind their art,” Castleberry said in a statement. “Our collaboration with Roku reinforces our commitment to not only visibility — but education, ownership, and long-term creative control.”


	Create The Writers Room titles currently streaming on the Roku Channel include “Sincerely Brad,” “Trailer Trash Magic,” “The Outtakes,” “The Errand” and “The Distinguished.” Additional titles from CTWR’s Five Film Fridays programming are slated to debut on the platform.

	The June 26 edition of Five Film Fridays featured “See You Soon,” written and directed by Jay Pendarvis Jr. and starring Asante Blackk and Isaiah John; “Halfrican,” created by Vic Mensa, directed by Edgar Esteves, and starring Mensa and Olly Sholotan; “Bubble Pop,” written and directed by Aeryn Michelle Williams and starring Emmy Raver-Lampman, Cailyn Rice and Marc Forgot; “Mirage,” written and directed by Jhanvi Motla, and starring Shavya Samala and Abhimanyu Katyal; and “Bukra,” written and directed by Alex Aljouni, starring Lamitta Saloum and Laith Nakli. 
	

	The next installment of Five Film Fridays will take place on Oct. 16 in Los Angeles, with a special edition expanding to New York City. Submissions are now open and close on Sept. 25, and for the first time, CTWR is accepting feature-length films.

	Maya Jackson, director of television development at 1Community, serves as head of programming for CTWR’s “Five Film Fridays”; Dwayne Davis joins the initiative as creative operations manager. Castleberry is represented by Khalid Jordan at M88.

schema.headline: The Roku Channel Expands Partnership With Create the Writers Room Initiative by Streaming ‘Five Film Fridays’ Selections

title: Sienna Spiro Plays Intimate Benefit Concert for North Shore Animal League
description: Fast-rising British singer Sienna Spiro played a special set at New York’s intimate Soho Sessions in support of the North Shore Animal League Americ
body:
Fast-rising British singer Sienna Spiro played a special set at New York’s intimate Soho Sessions in support of the North Shore Animal League America, a Long Island-based animal-rescue organization.

	The highly touted 20-year-old singer, who released her debut album “Visitor” earlier this month, played a set for an invite-only crowd of around 150 people, based heavily on the album but also livened things up with three covers, the ‘60s R&B chestnut “Hard to Handle” (also a hit single for the Black Crowes from their 1990 debut album) and Lauryn Hill’s 1999 classic “Doo Wop (Dat Ding),” as well as the somber standard “Autumn Leaves.”
	

	While she’s often (intimidatingly) compared with Adele, Spiro is an assured and nuanced singer with a dazzling range and ability to belt that belies her age — although she spoke at length about the influence Nina Simone has had on her singing, so she’s clearly studied the classics.


	The Soho Sessions are an ongoing series of intimate, invite-only concerts to raise funds and awareness for multiple charities, in this case the North Shore Animal League America, which even brought a pair of puppies to drive their point home (Spiro is pictured above with Soho Sessions founders Greg Williamson and Nicole Rechter). The organization describes itself as “the world’s largest and longest running no-kill animal rescue and adoption organization.”

	The concerts, which take place on a floor in a commercial building that once housed the hip-hop mecca Chung King Studios (where classics by Run-DMC, the Beastie Boys and Public Enemy were recorded), are organized by producers Williamson and Rechter, who have produced several successful concert series, including Love Rocks NYC (with John Varvatos) and the Talkhouse Jams. They estimate that they have helped raise more than $50 million for charity with their shows.
	

	Past performers include Keith Richards, Mavis Staples, Steve Earle, Gary Clark Jr., Taj Mahal, Yola, Susan Tedeschi, Lucius, Brittney Spencer and others — and each one benefits a different charity. Past beneficiaries have included Everytown for Gun Safety; Music Will, the largest nonprofit music program in the U.S. public school system; the National Alliance on Mental Illness / Hawaii (after the devastating fire in Lahaina); and others.

schema.headline: Sienna Spiro Plays Intimate Benefit Concert for North Shore Animal League

title: Trump Media’s Plan to Sell ‘Real-Time’ Access to President’s Truth Social Posts Draws Fire From GOP Lawmakers: ‘That Does Not Sound Appropriate’
description: Last week, Trump Media & Technology Group — which operates Truth Social, the U.S. president’s preferred platform for digital communications — said i
body:
Last week, Trump Media & Technology Group — which operates Truth Social, the U.S. president’s preferred platform for digital communications — said it will provide “immediate, verified access” to “the highest-ranking” accounts on Truth Social. That would let, say, financial institutions pay to get instant alerts about potentially market-moving statements from Donald Trump himself.

	TMTG execs have talked about charging Wall Street traders and investment firms up to $100,000 per month for faster access to the president’s posts, as first reported by the Financial Times.
	

	The Truth API program, TMTG said, “is designed for organizations most impacted by the cost of a delay in information. This includes high-frequency and algorithmic trading firms that require a low-latency, machine-readable feed rather than manual tracking.” The Truth API is able to deliver Truth Social posts to customers “in milliseconds.” It’s scheduled to become available Aug. 1, 2026, according to the company.

	Donald Trump is the largest shareholder in Trump Media, holding more than 40% of its shares through a revocable trust, according to a Bloomberg report this week.

	Now some Republican lawmakers are speaking out — and questioning the propriety of the new Trump Media initiative.

	“I think that’s wrong. It’s a form of buying access,” Sen. Bill Cassidy (R-La.) told The Hill. He added that families that are “struggling to make ends meet” would wonder, “Why would you reward those who are already doing well when we should do more for those who are struggling?’” GOP Sen. Susan Collins of Maine said about TMTG’s plan to sell access to Truth Social posts, “That does not sound appropriate,” according to the outlet.
	

	“The president is the president of the United States, he’s not in a position to use the position to enhance your own personal wealth. I hope he’s not thinking about that,” Sen. Lisa Murkowski (R-Alaska) told Bloomberg.

	Predictably, Democrats have also slammed the Truth API product. On Tuesday, Sen. Mark Warner (D-Va.), a member of the Committee on Banking, Housing and Urban Affairs, sent a letter to representatives of the financial services industry about his “concerns with Truth Social’s announcement that the service would offer advance access to the president’s posts on the platform for a substantial fee.”

	“This arrangement presents a serious risk to market integrity, creates a clear and unacceptable pathway for corruption, and undermines public confidence in the fair dissemination of market-moving government information — a crucial factor in maintaining stable and trustworthy financial markets,” Warner wrote in a letter to the Bank Policy Institute, the Securities Industry Financial Markets Association, the Managed Funds Association, the Financial Services Forum, the Principal Traders Group and the American Bankers Association.

	In remarks Tuesday on the Senate floor, Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) called Truth API “the exact definition of insider trading. It’s textbook pay-to-play. And this is what Donald Trump spends his time doing. And remember traders only need a millisecond head start to make a fortune. Just one millisecond and they can make a lot of money the way trading works these days. Every single day, we see a new example of Trump’s rampant corruption.”

	Schumer added by asking rhetorically, “How many American families do you think can afford $100,000 a month to get a leg up in the market?”

	Trump Media & Technology Group has pushed back on allegations that the Truth API program is improper. “With no apparent sense of irony, certain politicians falsely accuse us of anti-free market behavior while pressuring businesses into boycotting a product, all in a coordinated effort to harm a publicly traded company,” a TMTG spokesperson said in a statement to Axios. 

	TMTG said it has “already signed up customers ahead of the launch and is onboarding additional partners in the weeks ahead.”
	

	“Until now, no official, integrated API has existed, and firms that prioritize tracking influential Truth posts have relied on manual monitoring,” per the company’s announcement. “Truth API closes the gap for organizations that place a premium on immediate, verified access to information.”

	TMTG interim CEO Kevin McGurn previously said he expects the Truth API product to become “a meaningful, ongoing source of revenue.”

	“Markets already move on Truth Social posts,” McGurn said in announcing the initiative. “Truth API delivers a direct, licensed, real-time feed of the platform’s most market-moving Truths while advancing our strategy to monetize proprietary assets through a high-margin, recurring revenue stream. As adoption grows, we expect Truth API to become a meaningful, ongoing source of revenue for the company, creating lasting value for shareholders.” McGurn is a former Hulu and Vevo exec who took over running Trump Media after the sudden exit of Devin Nunes, the former U.S. congressman who had been TMTG’s CEO since 2022.

	Trump Media & Technology Group, which says its mission is “to end Big Tech’s assault on free speech by opening up the Internet and giving people their voices back,” has stayed firmly mired in red ink.

	For the first quarter of 2026, TMTG generated net sales of $871,200, up 6% year over year. The company reported a $405.9 million net loss and a $387.8 million adjusted EBITDA (earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization) loss for the first quarter of 2026. The vast bulk of the losses were “non-cash losses including unrealized losses on digital assets, digital assets pledged, and equity securities ($368.7 million), accreted interest ($11.5 million), and stock based compensation ($11.8 million),” the company said in a press release.

schema.headline: Trump Media’s Plan to Sell ‘Real-Time’ Access to President’s Truth Social Posts Draws Fire From GOP Lawmakers: ‘That Does Not Sound Appropriate’

title: 'Godzilla vs. Kong' Kaylee Hottle: Driver In Fatal Crash Not Impaired by Alcohol
description:
'Godzilla vs. Kong' Kaylee Hottle
                          Driver In Fatal Crash Wasn't Impaired
body:
'Godzilla vs. Kong' Kaylee Hottle
                          Driver In Fatal Crash Wasn't Impaired by Alcohol
                      
                  
                                                                        Published 
                              July 23, 2026
                            6:55 AM PDT
                                                                      |
                                                                Updated 
                                  July 23, 2026
                                8:26 AM PDT
                                    
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            The driver in the crash that killed "Godzilla vs. Kong" actress Kaylee Hottle was NOT impaired by alcohol ... though investigators in Maryland are still waiting to learn whether drugs were involved.
      
    
            The Frederick County Sheriff's Office tells TMZ ... they have ruled out alcohol impairment for the 19-year-old driver, but results from a drug test have not yet come back.
      
    
            As for whether the driver could face criminal charges ... the sheriff's office says "that is still under investigation."
      
    
            As TMZ previously reported ... Kaylee was a passenger in a 1995 Honda Accord when the driver veered off a road and slammed into a ditch. Authorities believe excessive speed was a factor in Tuesday's single-vehicle crash.
      
    
            The medical examiner determined Kaylee died from multiple blunt force injuries and ruled her death an accident. The driver suffered injuries not believed to be life-threatening ... while another passenger declined treatment at the scene.
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            Photos of the wrecked Honda showed catastrophic damage to the vehicle's front end ... including a crumpled hood, mangled bumper and a tire that appeared to have separated from its rim.
      
    
            Kaylee was a deaf actress best known for playing Jia in two 'Godzilla' films. She was 18.

title: 'The Odyssey' Viking Ship Allegedly Damaged During Filming, Owner Claims Studio Didn't Pay
description:
'The Odyssey'
                          Studio Skipped Bill For Damaged Viking Ship?!?
body:
'The Odyssey'
                          Studio Skipped Bill For Damaged Viking Ship?!?
                      
                  
                                                                        Published 
                              July 23, 2026
                            10:04 AM PDT
                                                                            
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            "The Odyssey" is a major blockbuster thanks to its impressive visuals, including a replica Viking ship ... but now the company that rented out the vessel claims it was damaged during filming ... and they say Universal hasn't paid for repairs.
      
    
            The Swedish non-profit Värmland Vikingaleden Association rented the studio its ship -- the Glad av Gillberga -- for the Christopher Nolan epic ... but the center's chairman, Peter Olausson, told multiple outlets the studio didn't pay the full bill, which includes costs for wear and tear.
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            He says all Universal paid was the rent fee and nothing else.
      
    
            Olausson blasted the studio ... saying the $6,000 he needs for repairs is peanuts, especially considering the flick raked in around $264,100,000 in ticket sales over its opening weekend.
      
    
            He says he hopes it was a simple overlook ... but has not heard back from Universal after sending several emails about the issue.
      
    
            We're out to the studio for comment ... so far, no word back.
      
      
              
            
              Related articles

schema.headline: 'The Odyssey' Viking Ship Allegedly Damaged During Filming, Owner Claims Studio Didn't Pay

title: Hunter Biden Suggests Some Republican Leaders Are Gay
description:
Hunter Biden on Republicans
                          I Won't Out Anyone, But Are You Asking If H
body:
Hunter Biden on Republicans
                          I Won't Out Anyone, But Are You Asking If Hawley, Miller Are Gay?!?
                      
                  
                                                                        Published 
                              July 23, 2026
                            8:08 AM PDT
                                                                            
      
    
        
                    
                  
      
    
            Hunter Biden says some of the biggest Republicans in Washington might be closeted ... and he's kinda naming names.
      
    
            Joe Biden's son sat down with Don Lemon to dish about why conservative politicians are so obsessed with gay people ... claiming it's because they're gay, too!
      
    
            Don asks Hunter point-blank if the late Lindsey Graham was part of the so-called closeted cabal. HB said it wasn't a secret, noting "everybody knew."
      
    
            As far as the rest of the list ... Hunter tries not to name names -- though he did bring up a backdoor route to mention a few prominent members of the Republican Party.
      

                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            Hunter didn't come out and say Senator Josh Hawley, Trump deputy chief of staff for policy Stephen Miller and Speaker of the House Mike Johnson are in the closet ... but bringing their names into the conversation in a roundabout way will certainly raise eyebrows.
      
    
            HB joked he was already knee-deep in enough lawsuits ... but he might be inviting some more here.
      
    
            Bottom line ... that "G" in GOP might stand for "gay" these days -- at least if you believe Hunter and Don!

title: Malcolm-Jamal Warner's Widow Gives Icy Interview Amid Legal War With His Mom
description:
Malcolm-Jamal Warner's Widow
                          Ice-Cold Interview Exchange Amid Family Le
body:
Malcolm-Jamal Warner's Widow
                          Ice-Cold Interview Exchange Amid Family Legal War
                      
                  
                                                                        Published 
                              July 23, 2026
                            7:55 AM PDT
                                                                      |
                                                                Updated 
                                  July 23, 2026
                                8:32 AM PDT
                                    
      
    
        
                    
                  
      
    
            Malcolm-Jamal Warner's widow is suing the late actor's estate for more than a million dollars ... a move that wasn't known when she sat for a recent interview -- but there were hints of family drama.
      
    
            Speaking with Gayle King ... Tenisha Warner was pretty icy when it came to her mother-in-law during a chat for "CBS Mornings" ... and she made it clear the two were NOT close when she was asked about their relationship.
      
    
            Tenisha says ... "The emotional capacity that I have can be very limited sometimes, you know? And so, I have to, for myself, decide, 'What do I have to give right now in this moment?' And whatever I have to give, the first person to get that is my daughter. And then the second is myself."
      
    
            TMZ broke the story ... Tenisha sued Pamela Warner in Georgia court this week over $1.2 million she claims she's owed from her alleged prenuptial agreement with the 'Cosby Show' star.
      

                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            It's unclear when Tenisha sat down with Gayle ... but CBS says the chat was before we first reported on her lawsuit.
      
    
            Both the suit and the interview come one year after Malcolm's July 2025 drowning death.
